description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21; eli5.html is the design doc; requires fz-rh2.21.8 landed. Closeout: documentation and stance-recording, plus the conformance sweep. Per project law, docs are present-tense facts about built subsystems, grounded in code — no plans, no history (the tickets carry history).

DOCS TO UPDATE (verify each claim against the landed code before writing):
- .agent/docs/type-specialization.md: the termination story currently cites bounded literal chains (int_lit collapse) as the convergence argument; the true argument is now: cumulative ReturnType joins (monotone), the widening-with-delay operator (finite chains; RETURN_WIDENING_DELAY with its corpus-derived value), atom-set finiteness (program text bounds atom chains), and rebase-on-ground-shift (per-epoch restarts). Describe the ascent/shift/rebase propagation rules as facts of the scheduler.
- .agent/docs/set-theoretic-types.md (or the axis table doc if named differently): the descr axis table — ints/floats are presence bits; atoms/brands/opaques/vars are finite-or-cofinite literal sets; lists are element-summaries (note the cons-fold normalization and suffix stability); record the Elixir alignment stance: numeric literals are VALUES (dispatch constants, lowered map keys), never types; literal numerics in type position widen to integer()/float() with the TYPE_NUMERIC_LITERAL_WIDENED warning.
- A fact-engine doc (create .agent/docs/fact-engine.md if none exists; check for an existing scheduler/facts doc first): the claim model — publishers/dirty/revision slots, settledness as per-fact status; waiting-extends vs concluding-replaces; is_cumulative content algebra; ascent vs ground-shift propagation; the rebase flag; where the only earned-any sites are (provider boundaries, unresolvable callable values). Keep it to observable, code-grounded behavior with file pointers.
- guides/ (user-facing): if any guide documents @type/@spec literal semantics or typed dispatch, update it for the widening stance with the digit example; if none exists, add the stance to whichever guide covers typespecs.
- eli5.html: delete it (it was the design sketch for this epic; the agent docs now carry the durable facts) — OR move its narrative into the PR description for the epic branch. Do not leave a stale design doc in the repo root.

CONFORMANCE SWEEP:
- Grep the tree for the removed vocabulary and confirm zero compiler2 references remain: widen_literal_flow, widen_for_recursive_spec_key, IntSet/FloatSet in compiler2 paths, observed_ty/flow_ty, merge_observed_ty (if it survived as the Option join, ensure naming reflects that), any unwrap_or-any defaults in jobs/semantic.rs.
- Re-run the N-derivation check from fz-rh2.21.4: full corpus + matrix with the widening-engaged assertion (zero engagements) and record the maximum observed ascent in the RETURN_WIDENING_DELAY doc-comment if it changed.
- Full verification: fresh uniquely-marked runs, cargo test --lib (with the documented skip) and the matrix suite, 0 new failures vs the epic baseline, zero warnings.
- Close out: comment on fz-rh2.21 with final diffstats per arc (the epic's goal was negative LoC with more capability — report the numbers), then review the fz-rh2.18.x tickets (18.6, 18.7, 18.8, 18.9 and children) against the landed model and comment on each: which are now structurally subsumed (candidates: any current-vs-settled hand-management that ascent/shift now owns), which remain live. Do NOT close 18.x tickets unilaterally — leave the assessment as comments for the maintainer.
Acceptance: docs accurate against code, sweep clean, one commit, epic ready to close.
title: closeout: docs, stances, conformance sweep, 18.x review
